details: The "Disability Aids and Appliances" scheme was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department. Telangana. The scheme aims to provide artificial limbs, wheelchairs, tricycles, and other fabricated appliances to registered construction workers who have lost limbs or sustained disabilities due to accidents.
benefits: Free distribution of artificial limbs, wheelchairs/tricycles, and fabricated appliances.
eligibility: The worker should be a registered building or construction worker with the Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
The disablement must have occurred due to an accident at the worksite or anywhere.

offline: " at the top of menu options.
Now click on the download button corresponding to the scheme name.
: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned Assistant Labour Officer.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

documents_required: Registration Card under the BOCW Act (Attested copy).
Doctor Certificate (issued by the Medical Board).
Medical Reports (issued by the Medical Board).

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Registered workers with the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board who have sustained disabilities due to accidents.

faqs.question: What benefits are provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides artificial limbs, wheelchairs/tricycles, and other fabricated appliances to eligible workers.

faqs.question: Is there any cost involved for the workers?
faqs.answer: No, all aids are provided free of charge.

faqs.question: What type of accidents are covered under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Accidents resulting in disability, whether occurring at the workplace or elsewhere, are covered.

faqs.question: Can a worker apply for this scheme if the accident happened before May 1, 2016?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only applicable for accidents that occurred on or after May 1, 2016.

faqs.question: Where will the aids be sourced from?
faqs.answer: Aids will be sourced from the Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India (ALIMCO), a Government of India organization.

faqs.question: How does one apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Submit an application form with the required documents to the Assistant Labour Officer.

faqs.question: Who verifies the application?
faqs.answer: The Assistant Labour Officer verifies the application before it is sent to the District Screening Committee.

faqs.question: What happens after the application is approved?
faqs.answer: The Deputy Commissioner of Labour will arrange for the supply of the necessary aids from ALIMCO.

faqs.question: Who oversees the implementation of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The Commissioner of Labour, Telangana, Hyderabad, and the Secretary & Chief Executive Officer of the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

faqs.question: Can the aid be collected in person?
faqs.answer: The aid will be arranged through the Hyderabad Regional Office of ALIMCO.
